author: shichishima
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. macOS / Emacs style cursor navigation (Ctrl+PNBF/AE) and Kill & Yank (Ctrl+KY) with full Markdown table support.

manifest.author: shichishima
manifest.description: macOS/Emacs-style cursor navigation (Ctrl+PNBF/AE) and Kill/Yank (Ctrl+KY) with full Markdown table support.
manifest.name: Universal Cursor Hotkeys

name: Universal Cursor Hotkeys

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Added - **Kill Region (Ctrl+W):** Cuts the selected region to the kill cache and system clipboard. Table-aware: single-cell only; no-op for multi-row or cross-cell selections. Selecting text that spans a `
` separator in Live Preview removes the separator along with the selected text. - **Delete Char (Ctrl+D):** Forward-deletes one character. In Live Preview table cells, stops at the cell boundary and joins in-cell sub-lines by removing the `
` tag when at the end of a non-last sub-line. - **Recenter (Ctrl+L):** Scrolls the view so the cursor line is centered on screen. Works in plain text and inside table cells. - **Page down / Page up:** Scrolls down or up one page, moving the cursor with it. - **Select all (table-aware):** When the cursor is inside a table cell, selects only the content of that cell rather than the entire document. - **Smart Home in nested blockquotes:** HOME now navigates inside nested blockquotes (`>>`, `> > >`, etc.), recognizing inner markup (headings, lists, task lists, ordered lists, footnotes) in the same way as regular lines. - **Smart join (default OFF):** When Kill Line joins the next line, strips everything to the left of the next line's content start — blockquote markers, list markers, indentation, and (with Smart home (advanced) ON) headings and footnotes. Controlled independently of Smart home; disabled when Smart home (standard) is OFF. ### Changed - **Smart home (standard):** Kill Line join behavior is no longer tied to this setting. Description updated to clarify the Windows Home / macOS Cmd+← analogy.

releases.author: shichishima
releases.description: fix: address Obsidian plugin review feedback

releases.author: shichishima
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes                                                                     
- **Kill chain reset:** Yank (Ctrl+Y) and cursor-movement commands now correctly break the consecutive-kill accumulation. Previously, performing a Yank or moving the cursor did not reset the kill chain, causing subsequent Kill Line (Ctrl+K) to unexpectedly append to the previous kill.
- **Yank cursor position:** Fixed an issue where yanking text that ends with a newline into a Live Preview table cell caused the cursor to jump to the next cell instead of staying within the current cell.

releases.author: shichishima
releases.description:
- Add kill-line & yank command
  - Support both inside and outside of cells (both from/to plain text and tables)
  - Support multiline operations
  - Does not support kill-ring (integrated with system clipboard)
  - Leading whitespace on the next line is included in the kill at end-of-line (when Smart home is ON)
- Support HOME/END movement in Source Mode table cells

releases.author: shichishima
releases.description:
- Settings screen with four toggle options: Visual line movement, Smart home (standard), Smart home (advanced), and Cross-row navigation.
- Smart home now works inside table cells, skipping in-cell line boundaries.

releases.author: shichishima
releases.description:
- Fix down-navigation bug for multi-line cells in the bottom row.
- Fix bug preventing moving out of a table at the end of the file.

releases.author: shichishima
releases.description: fix: footnote smart-HOME skipping to ch=0 due to Live Preview widget artifact

releases.author: shichishima
releases.description:
- fix: Prevent cursor from getting stuck in empty cells
- fix: Adjust behavior at the bottom row of tables at the end of documents

releases.author: shichishima
releases.description:
- Visual-line navigation via Ctrl+A/E for non-table text.
- Logical-line navigation via Ctrl+A/E within table cells.
- Up/Down cursor movement support inside table cells.
- Add command: "Select all"
